steps.title: Simplify both equations
steps.content:
The first equation gives $y=-x^2$. The second equation gives $y=8x+16$.
$$x^2+y+3=3$$
$$y=-x^2$$
$$8x+16-y=0$$
$$y=8x+16$$

steps.title: Set the y-values equal
steps.content:
At the intersection, the two expressions for $y$ are equal.
$$-x^2=8x+16$$
$$x^2+8x+16=0$$

steps.title: Solve for x
steps.content: Factor the quadratic. $$(x+4)^2=0$$ $$x=-4$$ The answer is B.
